skill.name: excalidraw-diagram-generator
skill.description: Generate Excalidraw diagrams from natural language descriptions. Creates flowcharts, mind maps, architecture diagrams, and more as .excalidraw JSON files.
skill.summary: Transform natural language descriptions into professional Excalidraw diagrams including flowcharts, mind maps, architecture diagrams, and ER diagrams.
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged documentation content as executable code. All 279 'external_commands' findings in .md files are false positives - these are code examples in documentation, not actual executable code. Python helper scripts perform legitimate file operations for diagram management. No malicious patterns detected.

medium_findings.title: Static analyzer false positives in documentation
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown documentation files as containing executable shell commands. All findings in .md files reference code examples within documentation blocks, not actual executable code. These are instructional examples showing users how to use the skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown files cannot execute shell commands. Static analyzer pattern matching triggered on backtick-enclosed code examples in documentation.

low_findings.title: File write operations in helper scripts
low_findings.description: Python helper scripts (add-arrow.py, add-icon-to-diagram.py, split-excalidraw-library.py) perform file write operations to modify Excalidraw diagram files. This is legitimate functionality for the skill but requires user awareness.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Scripts perform documented file operations on user-specified Excalidraw files. No hidden or unauthorized file access detected.

low_findings.title: URLs in documentation and templates
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to excalidraw.com and libraries.excalidraw.com - official Excalidraw resources. These are reference links in documentation, not active network calls in executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are legitimate Excalidraw domain references in documentation context. No evidence of data exfiltration or unauthorized network activity.

content.user_title: Create Excalidraw Diagrams from Natural Language
content.value_statement: Transform ideas into visual diagrams without manual drawing. Generate professional flowcharts, mind maps, and architecture diagrams as editable Excalidraw files.
actual_capabilities: Generate flowcharts from process descriptions with sequential steps and decision points
Create mind maps with central topics, main branches, and sub-topics
Produce architecture diagrams showing system components and their interactions
Build ER diagrams with entities, attributes, relationships, and cardinality notations
Generate sequence diagrams with actors, lifelines, and message flows
Create class diagrams with classes, attributes, methods, and inheritance relationships
limitations: Output is Excalidraw JSON format only - requires Excalidraw application to view
Complex diagrams with many elements may require multiple iterations to refine
Diagram layout is programmatic - manual adjustment may be needed for optimal appearance
Icon insertion requires separate helper scripts and downloaded icon libraries

use_cases.title: Software Architecture Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate system architecture diagrams showing microservices, data flows, and component relationships for technical documentation.
use_cases.target_user: Software architects and developers documenting system design

use_cases.title: Process Visualization
use_cases.description: Create flowcharts and swimlane diagrams to document business processes, workflows, and standard operating procedures.
use_cases.target_user: Business analysts and process improvement specialists

use_cases.title: Database Design
use_cases.description: Generate ER diagrams from database schema descriptions to visualize entity relationships and data models.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Flowchart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a flowchart showing the user login process: start with user entering credentials, validate against database, if valid show dashboard, if invalid show error message, end.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple sequential process with one decision point

prompt_templates.title: Mind Map Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a mind map for 'Project Management' with central topic in the center. Main branches should include: Planning, Execution, Monitoring, and Closure. Add 2-3 sub-topics under each branch.
prompt_templates.scenario: Concept hierarchy for brainstorming or study materials

prompt_templates.title: System Architecture Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an architecture diagram for a web application with: React frontend, API Gateway, Authentication Service, User Service, and PostgreSQL database. Show request flow from user to frontend to gateway to services to database.

prompt_templates.title: Sequence Diagram with Multiple Actors
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a sequence diagram for an e-commerce checkout: Customer, Frontend, Payment Service, and Inventory Service. Show: add to cart, initiate checkout, validate payment, reserve inventory, confirm order, send confirmation email.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex interaction flow across multiple system components

output_examples.input: Create a simple flowchart with Start, Process Data, Decision (Valid?), Yes leads to Save, No leads to Error, both lead to End.
output_examples.output: Generates .excalidraw file with 6 rectangle elements (Start, Process Data, Decision diamond, Save, Error, End) connected by directional arrows showing the flow path.

output_examples.input: Create a mind map for 'Machine Learning' with branches for Supervised, Unsupervised, and Reinforcement learning.
output_examples.output: Produces .excalidraw file with central ellipse containing 'Machine Learning' and three main branches with labeled rectangles, each containing sub-topic rectangles.

best_practices: Start with a clear description of diagram type, key elements, and their relationships before generation
Review generated diagrams and request specific adjustments for element positioning or styling
Use template files as starting points for common diagram types to ensure consistent formatting
anti_patterns: Do not request diagrams without specifying the diagram type - results may not match expectations
Avoid overly complex single diagrams with 20+ elements - split into multiple focused diagrams
Do not expect automatic layout optimization - programmatic layouts may require manual refinement

faq.question: What file format does this skill output?
faq.answer: The skill generates .excalidraw JSON files that can be opened directly in the Excalidraw application (web or desktop). These are standard Excalidraw format files.

faq.question: Can I edit the generated diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. Once generated, open the .excalidraw file in Excalidraw to manually adjust positions, colors, text, or add additional elements as needed.

faq.question: What diagram types are supported?
faq.answer: Flowcharts, mind maps, architecture diagrams, ER diagrams, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, data flow diagrams, and swimlane business flow diagrams.

faq.question: Do I need to install Excalidraw to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need Excalidraw to view and edit the output files. Excalidraw is free and available as a web application at excalidraw.com or as a desktop application.

faq.question: Can the skill add icons to diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes, helper scripts (add-icon-to-diagram.py) are included for adding icons from Excalidraw libraries. You must download icon libraries separately from libraries.excalidraw.com.

faq.question: How accurate are the diagram layouts?
faq.answer: Layouts are programmatically generated and functional. Complex diagrams may require manual adjustment in Excalidraw for optimal visual appearance and element spacing.
